Category: Public technology company (video communications / collaboration platform)
Overview of the Company: Zoom Communications, Inc. provides a unified communications platform centered on high-quality video conferencing, webinars, chat, and collaboration tools. Originally known for seamless video meetings, it has evolved into an AI-first work platform integrating agentic AI, workflow automation, contact center, and workplace solutions. It serves millions of users globally with a freemium model, strong enterprise adoption, and focus on reliability, security, and innovation in hybrid/remote work.
Company Headquarters: 55 Almaden Boulevard, 6th Floor, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
Countries Operating In: United States; United Kingdom; Australia; Japan; Germany; France; Singapore; Netherlands; India; Canada
Office Locations: Denver, CO; Santa Barbara, CA; London, UK; Amsterdam, NL; Tokyo, JP; Sydney, AU; Singapore, SG; Bengaluru, IN; Paris, FR; Berlin, DE
Vision: To deliver an AI-first work platform for human connection.
Mission: To make video communications frictionless and secure while delivering happiness to users.
Values: Care; Innovation; Customer obsession; Integrity; Collaboration; Diversity & inclusion
Interesting Facts: Founder Eric Yuan was the VP of Engineering at Webex before leaving to start Zoom; The platform successfully hosted over 3.3 trillion annual meeting minutes during its peak growth phase.
Recent News: Feb 2026: FY2026 revenue $4.869B (+4.4%), strong margins, share buybacks; April 2026: Appointed Russell Dicker as Chief Product Officer for AI-first strategy; Ongoing AI Companion and agentic AI expansions; March 2026: Enterprise agentic AI platform updates
Regulatory & Compliance Status: HIPAA Compliant; GDPR Compliant; SOC2 Type II; FedRAMP Authorized; ISO 27001
Legal Issues / Controversies: FTC settlement over security claims (Resolved); Ongoing scrutiny regarding data routing and AI training privacy
ESG Practices or Ratings: Net-zero emissions target 2030; Zoom Cares social impact fund; SASB Software & IT Services compliant reporting
Supply Chain Dependencies: AWS (Core Infrastructure); Oracle Cloud; NVIDIA (AI GPUs); Logitech (Hardware Partnership)
Geopolitical Risks: US-China trade relations (Historical data routing concerns); EU Digital Markets Act; Global AI sovereignty laws
Macro Risks: Bundled competition (Microsoft/Google); Global economic slowdown impacting IT budgets; Shift back to in-office work
Ethical Sourcing Practices: Supplier Code of Conduct; Modern Slavery Act Transparency; E-waste recycling for hardware bundles
Customer testimonial: Zoom has become the essential OS for our global team collaboration. - CIO, Walmart

Pain Points Being Addressed: Friction in remote collaboration; High cost of legacy hardware systems; Fragmented business communication tools; Lack of AI-driven meeting productivity

Top Customers by Client Segments: Global Enterprise (Walmart, HSBC); Educational Institutions (Harvard, ASU); Healthcare Providers; SMB Commercial Sector

Unique Differentiators: Proprietary video compression tech; AI Companion included at no extra cost; Seamless "Workplace" integration; High-fidelity mobile performance
Competitive Advantages: High brand equity ("Zoom" as a verb); Massive network effects; Agile AI deployment; Strong free-to-paid conversion funnel
Weaknesses / Gaps in Offering: Intense competition from bundled suites (Microsoft/Google); Post-pandemic growth deceleration; Legacy perception as "just a meeting app"
